LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STOKES, CORY, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS' APPLICATION FOR ORDER: A) GRANTING PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS SETTLEMENT AND PROVISIONALLY CERTIFYING THE SETTLEMENT CLASS; B) APPROVING FORM AND MANNER OF NOTICE TO PROVIDE TO THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT CLASS AND DIRECTING THAT A NEUTRAL THIRD PARTY GIVE SUCH NOTICE TO THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT CLASS; C) APPROVING HEFFLER CLAIMS GROUP, A NEUTRAL THRID PARTY, AS CLAIMS ADMINISTRATOR; D) SETTING A HEARING FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT AND AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS TO CLASS COUNSEL AND ENHANCEMENT TO CLASS REPRESENTATIVE; E) IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 23, 2014. THERE ARE SEVERAL PIECES OF INFORMATION THAT THE PARTIES MUST PROVIDE BEFORE THE COURT CAN PRELIMINARILY APPROVE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. COUNSEL HAS NOT PROVIDED DATA ON HOW MANY WORKWEEKS EACH OF THE 57 ACCOUNT MANAGERS COMPLETED, NOR HAS IT PROVIDED THE "DISTRIBUTION FORMULA" TO EXPLAIN THE VALUE OF EACH WORKWEEK. IT IS ALSO UNCLEAR WHAT WILL HAPPEN IN THE EVENT THAT FEW PEOPLE SIGN UP AND THERE ARE FUNDS LEFTOVER FROM THE NET SETTLEMENT AMOUNT. IT APPEARS THAT THE CY PRES ORGANIZATION WILL ONLY RECEIVE UNCASHED CHECKS, NOT NECESSARILY SURPLUS FUNDS (IF ANY) FROM THE NET SETTLEMENT AMOUNT. IT IS UNCLEAR WHETHER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT TO HAVE THE SURPLUS FUNDS BE DISTRIBUTED AMONG ALL CLASS MEMBERS, BE PASSED TO CY PRES, OR REVERT TO DEFENDANT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/EHG

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED REGARDING THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE COURT IN ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CORY STOKES, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS' APPLICATION FOR ORDER: A) GRANTING PRELIMINARY APPROVAL OF CLASS SETTLEMENT AND PROVISIONALLY CERTIFYING THE SETTLEMENT CLASS; B) APPROVING FORM AND MANNER OF NOTICE TO PROVIDE TO THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT CLASS AND DIRECTING THAT A NEUTRAL THIRD PARTY GIVE SUCH NOTICE TO THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT CLASS; C) APPROVING HEFFLER CLAIMS GROUP, A NEUTRAL THRID PARTY, AS CLAIMS ADMINISTRATOR; D) SETTING A HEARING FOR FINAL APPROVAL OF THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT AND AWARD OF ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS TO CLASS COUNSEL AND ENHANCEMENT TO CLASS REPRESENTATIVE IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 20, 2014 AND DEEMED SUBMITTED ON THAT DATE. THE COURT TO LOOK AT THE SUPPLEMENTAL MEMORANDUM FILED WITH THE COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Continued To Jul-15-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to the Compliance Hearing Re Class Settlement Funds Distribution (Per 12/15/14 Court Order), since the declaration of Ms. Pappas shows that the distribution of funds is not yet complete as had apparently been contemplated by paragraph 8 of the December 15, 2014 order granting final approval of the class settlement, there will be a further compliance hearing on July 15, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302 to confirm that all settlement funds have been distributed. A status report needs to be filed no later than five court days prior to the further compliance hearing. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)

Notice Of Application And Plaintiff'S Application For Order: A) Granting Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Provisionally Certifying The Settlement Class; B) Approving Form And Manner Of Notice To Provide To The Proposed Settlement Class And Directing That A Neutral Third Party Give Such Notice To The Proposed Settlement Class; C) Approving Heffler Claims Group, A Neutral Thrid Party, As Claims Administrator; D) Setting A Hearing For Final Approval Of The Proposed Settlement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel And Enhancement To Class Representative; E) ...
Off Calendar Sep-09-2014 Continued To Sep-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF STOKES, CORY, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL OTHERS' Application For Order: A) Granting Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement And Provisionally Certifying The Settlement Class; B) Approving Form And Manner Of Notice To Provide To The Proposed Settlement Class And Directing That A Neutral Third Party Give Such Notice To The Proposed Settlement Class; C) Approving Heffler Claims Group, A Neutral Thrid Party, As Claims Administrator; D) Setting A Hearing For Final Approval Of The Proposed Settlement And Award Of Attorneys' Fees And Costs To Class Counsel And Enhancement To Class Representative; E)
is continued to September 23, 2014. There are several pieces of information that the parties must provide before the Court can preliminarily approve the settlement agreement. Counsel has not provided data on how many workweeks each of the 57 Account Managers completed, nor has it provided the "distribution formula" to explain the value of each workweek.
It is also unclear what will happen in the event that few people sign up and there are funds leftover from the Net Settlement Amount. It appears that the cy pres organization will only receive uncashed checks, not necessarily surplus funds (if any) from the Net Settlement Amount. It is unclear whether the settlement agreement to have the surplus funds be distributed among all class members, be passed to cy pres, or revert to Defendant. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/eg
